title: Adapt a qualitative identity study to instructors' adoption of generative AI

research_logic: Use purposive sampling to capture variation in professional identity.
Generate semi-structured interview material around critical incidents.
Develop themes iteratively through coding and memoing.
Use negative cases and researcher reflexivity to refine interpretation.
strengths: Sampling is tied to theoretical variation rather than convenience alone.
Critical incidents connect abstract identity claims to situated evidence.
Negative cases prevent themes from becoming overly uniform.
context_bound_elements: The original profession has different institutional norms.
The original interview guide assumes a stable professional identity category.
The researcher's relationship to participants differs.

user_project.topic: How community-college instructors interpret generative-AI adoption in relation to teaching identity and professional responsibility.
available_materials: Access to instructors at two community colleges.
Ability to conduct and transcribe interviews.
constraints: Researcher works in an adjacent educational role.
Sensitive concerns about institutional policy and job security.

required_adaptation_moves.reference_move: Sample participants across theoretically meaningful identity positions.
required_adaptation_moves.required_user_action: Define variation in discipline, employment status, and AI-policy exposure before recruitment.

required_adaptation_moves.reference_move: Use critical incidents to ground identity interpretation.
required_adaptation_moves.required_user_action: Ask for concrete teaching episodes involving AI rather than only general attitudes.

required_adaptation_moves.reference_move: Treat the researcher's professional familiarity as unproblematic access.
required_adaptation_moves.required_user_action: Document positionality, role boundaries, and how prior assumptions are handled during analysis.

required_claim_boundaries: Themes describe contextualized interpretations among the selected participants.
The study may develop concepts or process insights without estimating prevalence.
forbidden_inferences: Most community-college instructors hold the identified view.
Interview themes prove that institutional policy causes identity change.
A small purposive sample is statistically representative.
